known_issues.description: Timeline segmentation is deterministic but does not guarantee semantic boundaries aligned with speech or sentence structure.
Audio extraction assumes presence of a usable audio stream; edge cases (silent tracks, corrupted streams) may not be fully validated.
No signal for audio clarity, noise level, or speech presence is included in output artifacts.
Subtitle streams are detected but not used to enhance timeline or downstream transcription alignment.

contract_assumptions: timeline.json contains strict segment_id, start, end, duration with no overlap
audio/source_full.wav exists only if audio stream is valid
timeline fully covers most of the video duration
segment ordering is stable and deterministic
merge_risks: Timeline segmentation may not align well with speech boundaries for STT module
Audio file format assumptions (wav) may not match STT engine expectations
Segment duration may be too short or too long for optimal transcription accuracy
integration_notes: Downstream modules must treat timeline as authoritative and not modify boundaries
STT module should not assume semantic segmentation from timeline
Audio path should be validated before passing to transcript module
